48.
49.
50. Coffee and cat tax
item.dc:creator: FailureisaPedagogy

item.title: James Webb be working... and wow...
item.description:
NASA has just announced the alignment of the James Webb Telescope. And we have the first picture taken from the working machine and... Holy Cow! Look at that?!?! So what actually happened today? The alignment of the hexagons that make up the mirrors on James Webb have been done. Basically they turned this... Front Page Edit: Space... it’s so awesome! Thank you usersub for loving it. Will say- the picture I used were the standard res ones available at first. One of the comments below has a high res version of the above and it’s like... HOLY MOTHER OF GOD!!! Yeah, that good.
Into this....
They have brought everything into focus. See that star? It’s nothing important. It’s a random star chosen from the Ursa Major constellation. Why that one? Banana for scale- it is 100 times fainter than the limit you can see with the naked eye. That’s how faint it is. And this is who Webb sees it. It collects that much light. And indeed look at the middle... there is a ‘bleed’. The amount of light gathered has caused a bleed. That’s amazing.
But it gets more amazing. The data suggests Webb is doing better than we expected. How much better? Visualise it this way. The sky is 360 degrees across. Objects can be seen based on the space they take up. The Moon is half a degree for example. To see that start like that? Means Webb is currently clocking a staggering 0.0000194 degrees resolution. We can see objects that small, that well. Look at it. Look at the details behind the star. And this is the work from ONE detector on Webb. The other stuff is working and seems fine. But let’s take a minute to go wow... https://www.nasa.gov/press-release/nasa-s-webb-reaches-alignment-milestone-optics-working-successfully https://www.space.com/james-webb-space-telescope-alignment-photo Anyway- Hey, I do this stuff because I love it and that’s that. But if you feel like buying me a coffee for it, you can click on the link below. https://www.buymeacoffee.com/Thefeck A full list of the posts done on Imgur over the years. https://imgur.com/gallery/aTASLQH
item.dc:creator: thefeckamisigningupwithfacebook

item.title: Carefully curated, easy-to-read Ukraine updates with sources, for people lacking time but wanting to be informed.
item.description:
Ukraine gained a complete victory in their case against Russia in the United Nations International Court of Justice. The ICJ issued an order (binding under international law) to Russia to immediately stop the invasion. Even the Chinese judge voted in Ukraine's favor. https://www.icj-cij.org/en/case/182 https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/world-court-rule-emergency-measures-ukraine-vs-russia-case-2022-03-16/
The direct quote: "Russian people will always be able to distinguish true patriots from traitors and just spit them out like a fly that accidentally flew into their mouth. Such a natural and necessary self-purification of the society will only strengthen our country." – Vladimir Putain It's pretty easy to read between the lines – because there aren't any lines to read between when you say things like "self-purification of the society will only strengthen our country."
There's not much to say about this one except "fuck russia". https://www.pravda.com.ua/news/2022/03/16/7331956/ https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/16/europe/ukraine-mariupol-bombing-theater-intl/index.html
The action is mostly symbolic in nature, but it's rare to see our two useless parties actually agree on something. https://www.reuters.com/world/us/us-senate-unanimously-condemns-putin-war-criminal-2022-03-15/ https://www.usnews.com/news/world/articles/2022-03-15/u-s-senate-unanimously-condemns-putin-as-war-criminal ...Also unanimously (though not related to Ukraine at all) the U.S. Senate also passed a bill making daylight saving time permanent starting in 2023. https://www.cnn.com/2022/03/16/politics/daylight-saving-time-senate-permanent-2023/index.html
Ukraine has begun attempts to not just defend against, but to push back the Russian invaders, most notably in Kyiv and at the Kherson air base. https://www.wsj.com/articles/ukraine-mounts-counteroffensive-to-drive-russians-back-from-kyiv-key-cities-11647428858 https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone/44780/ukraine-strikes-back-barrage-leaves-russian-occupied-kherson-airbase-in-flames
https://kyivindependent.com/ As with previous iterations of this same post, this is obviously coming from a completely Ukrainian source, but so far appears to be accurate. Russia still has released any statistics since March 2.
Stories of this one have been circulating for a short while already, but last night (this morning?) it was confirmed. Major General Oleg Mityaev was the commander of the Russian army’s 150th motorized rifle division. He was killed at Kyiv with 7 members of the Dzerzhinsky division, special operations that are under Putain's direct control. https://abcnews4.com/news/nation-world/ukraine-fourth-russian-general-killed-in-invasion-oleg-mityaev-mariupol-russian-invasion-vladimir-putin-war-volodymyr-zelenskyy-kyiv-lyiv https://www.mirror.co.uk/news/world-news/russian-general-killed-trying-storm-26478770
DESTROYS* (oops, typo) Oryx confirms at least 15 Russian helicopters were destroyed by Ukrainian artillery and air strikes: - 3 Mi-8 Transport helicopters - 2 Mi-24V/P attack helicopters - 2 Mi-35M attack helicopters - 3 Mi-24/35 (unsure of type) helicopters - 1 Mi-28 helicopter - 4 Ka-52 attack helicopters - 16 other unknown-type helicopters https://www.thedrive.com/the-war-zone/44780/ukraine-strikes-back-barrage-leaves-russian-occupied-kherson-airbase-in-flames Images to follow.
Ukraine can now cut off from the Russian power grid. This also helps to combat the widespread power outages caused by Russian attacks, and in the (hopeful) future should help allow import and export of power to the benefit of both Ukraine and EU member countries. https://www.bbc.com/news/live/world-europe-60746557 https://finance.yahoo.com/news/ukraine-connected-european-energy-grid-134720811.html
From personal statements made by members of the 41st combined arms army that deployed to Ukraine at the start of the invasion. Not just your regular enlisted men, but officers are rejecting orders to their commanders, using language such as that it is "impossible" to redeploy, citing issues such as lack of communications and technical means, as well as claiming (accurately) that they would be "cannon fodder". https://euromaidanpress.com/2022/03/16/russian-soldiers-are-refusing-to-redeploy-to-ukraine-citing-reasons-including-unwillingness-to-become-cannon-fodder-photographic-proof/ Note: The emphasis is on *RE*-deploy. These troops had already retreated from Ukraine after heavy losses.
The upcoming leaks will apparently largely pertain to what's being described as "Russian business". There are some pretty serious implications and this may affect, err, let's just say... some politicians in the United States (and other countries too). And they hacked into Russian streaming services and television, broadcasting independent news, and even videos of war crimes. https://www.rferl.org/a/russian-tv-hacked-ukraine-anonymous/31740663.html They also hacked Roskomnadzor, Russia's censorship agency. https://www.ibtimes.com/anonymous-hacks-roskomnadzor-leaks-files-proving-russia-controls-narrative-about-its-3433307
I'll share a couple minutes of the presentation below. Disclaimer: It's far from the worst footage I've seen coming out of Ukraine, but it's still graphic, disturbing, and heartbreaking.
https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/europe/russia-ukraine-war-deaths-chernihiv-b2037094.html https://www.bbc.com/news/live/world-europe-60746557 Video will be included below.

item.description: source: https://www.toisthe.com/2022/03/melitopol-mayor-rescued-special-operation-putin-humaliatiated.html EDIT NEWS CORRECTION: Ukraine handed over nine captured Russian soldiers to secure the freedom of the mayor of the city of Melitopol, who was detained last week, the Interfax Ukraine news agency quoted a senior official as saying on Wednesday. https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-forces-have-released-mayor-city-melitopol-kyiv-2022-03-16/
item.dc:creator: theshopliftingseagull

item.title: I made my dream dress for St Patrick’s Day!
item.description:
This dress has been tumbling around in my mind for years! I finally felt like I had enough skill to recreate it and found the perfect fabrics in my stash. Couldn’t have been more perfect for St Patrick’s Day!
I used quilting cotton blenders here to get the shades right. It’s actually surprisingly difficult to find coordinating fabric that’s the same colour but a different or inverted pattern. I had a third fabric instead of the gold but when I looked at it from a distance it didn’t contrast well enough to the stripes so I ditched it and bought some gold lame fabric. It’s the absolute worst to sew with and you can’t press it or it shrivels up like cellophane in heat, fuck that shit, never sewing with it again!
This was my inspiration, a 1960s dress from the designer Lanz. You can kinda see why it’s been on my mind for years! Mine is unfortunately less striking. Alas, the right fabric was almost impossible to find, so the green was the best I could do. I tried to be as faithful as I could to the original but I thought a pleated skirt would work really nicely and my neckline might be a bit lower than theirs. Also I just realised the waist has piping. Shit.
My back is different too because I wanted the option of wearing a bra with it but found I didn’t really need it because it’s very supportive. I constructed the lining to have an under-bust seam and I contoured it a little closer to my body to hold me in. Also as far as internals go, I added two columns of zip tie boning from armpit going to centre front. Can I just say one thing I didn’t expect was how difficult this dress is to get into because of the crossover straps? Absolute nightmare, I look like a possessed green gremlin when I’m getting dressed.
You can kind of see the fabric that was gonna be on the bodice used as my pocket lining. I found a new technique to sew inseam pockets and oh my god it’s like leagues better than whatever the hell mess I was doing before. It basically involves sewing the whole pocket before insertion, then sewing it on one side, clipping corners and flipping it out, then sewing skirt panels together in one fell swoop. Yep, that settles it, I’m awful at explaining things.
Regardless, this dress was a real labour of love for me and surprisingly one of my hardest makes yet. I made two test runs! I would have nailed the fit too if not for that stupid flimsy gold fabric that has zero structure behind it. At least I know for next time! Friends don’t let friends sew with lame fabric. Never again. Anyways, I made this dress over a series of several livestreams over at https://twitch.tv/carbonchic ! Feel free to join me some time if you wanna see how I make stuff or chat sewing or heckle me or whatever :)
item.dc:creator: CarbonChic

item.dc:creator: Rubikx107

item.title: Screw anyone that thinks kids are turned gay by what they're taught.
item.description:
I've taken this recent attack on mentioning gay people in a positive light to kids very personally. I don't want any kid to go through what I've been going through. To think there's something wrong with them and that it's their fault. It's cruelty. As far as I'm concerned, anyone that thinks this way is actively wanting to kill these kids simply for not aligning with whatever flavour of Sky Daddy's word they hold.
Edit: I'm sure it's obvious, but Twitter is me https://twitter.com/PoliPan6/status/1504075645579497473 And thanks to everyone who's been commenting in support. It honestly has helped lift my mood a lot, and I didn't expect that. It's nice to get that confirmation that people care, despite my environment. https://gofund.me/3541e45f I think I also got my first donation because of this post, and it's brought tears to my eyes. Thank you.
item.dc:creator: CharunDrakarus

item.title: Eat the rich!
item.description: SOURCE: https://dbr.gov.ua/news/dbr-peredalo-na-potrebi-armii-odin-mln-dolariv-ssha-viluchenih-pri-sprobi-vivezennya-ih-za-kordon TRANSLATION: Investigators from the State Bureau of Investigation confiscated gold and foreign currency worth about $ 1 million from an employee of the Ukrainian embassy in the EU in an attempt to smuggle it abroad. And the funds are transferred to the state budget and can be used for the needs of the Armed Forces of Ukraine. At the Yahodyn checkpoint of the Volyn customs of the State Customs Service on February 28 last year, DBR investigators inspected a diplomatic minibus in which almost 68,000 euros, more than 140,000 US dollars, more than 12 kilograms of gold bars, gold jewelry with a total weight of 13 8 kg, as well as a large number of cigarettes. An embassy employee tried to take all this abroad under the guise of diplomatic mail. Based on the court decision, cash, jewelry and gold worth about $ 1 million were confiscated. The court found the representative of the embassy guilty of committing an offense under Part 1 of Art. 483 of the Criminal Code of Ukraine and fined him UAH 26.8 million.

item.title: Whoops
item.description: Currently we’re having the bathroom renovated, part of which means putting in a new window. The builder asks “What sort of privacy glass do you want” “Not sure really, maybe the same as before, but then again the neighbours always say they san see right through aha!” “Yeah… they can, the glass is in the wrong way…” The bathroom glass is textured, similar to bubble wrap, with the idea that the bobbles are facing out so we can see out (partly) and no one can see in. The glass was there when we moved in around 7 years ago and we couldn’t see anything out, so assumed the view from in was obscured too. Not. At. All. Turn out, the whole village may have seen my cock and balls.

item.dc:creator: reformedxile

item.title: Homemade Stitchdown Boots w/ Vibram soles
item.description:
Finished product first, not the best angle
Finished, with a coat of Saphir Pate de Luxe, that stuff smells so good.
Vibram 2442(?) half soles with leather soles and heel stacks, more on this choice down below.
Project begins here. This is my 4th attempt at a pair of boots; pairs #2 and 3 were black in colour and you can see one in the top left corner. They didn't come out the way I wanted them to, so I didn't finish them properly. I'll try making some black boots again after this. I had this amount of 3-4oz veg tan calfskin leftover from a previous project, this is only about $15 worth of leather. I had to convert the tongues from a 1 piece design into 3 pieces in order for them to fit.
Pieces clicked and ready for dyeing. Using Saddle Tan colour this time.
I had lots of this woven medical tape lying around, because I accidentally stabbed a box cutter through my palm a month ago and had to go get some stitches. It was pretty impressively strong, so I decided to reinforce parts of the shoes with it. IDK what I'm doing, just mucking around really. There are some proper reinforcement tapes out there for shoes, but the shops that sell them are price gouging and they run up to $35/roll, f that.
Pieces dyed. I used a little rubbing alcohol to dilute and give some vehicle to the dye, which was kind of stupid and didn't work the way I wanted it to in the end. It eventually dries and lightens up and evens out after this pic.
I only do a couple seams on the home sewing machine, including the French bound seam on the topline. For everything else, I use the sewing machine to punch holes, and then I sew everything up by hand. This is just a plain straight stitch foot, and the only thing I've done here is put a leather needle in, that's it. I'm still looking for a proper post bed sewing machine in my area, but I haven't come across one yet.
Some more of that woven tape. The leather back stay piece on these is sewn to the inside over this, then over on the outside, and the end result is astonishingly strong.
This is polypropelene anti-stretch tape, proper tape intended for shoemaking, it was like $35 a roll. Some supplies in this hobby are maddeningly costly.
Lots of hand sewing, took a couple days. Thread is tex 70 weight here. The backs of the tongues were lined in goatskin for the f of it.
Both sides ready to close up
Shoes have been pre-lasted here, they take on a good amount of shape. That's the lasts, to the left. They need talc before they go back on for the final lasting, so they can slip off the lasts later. Talcum powder that isn't corn starch is awfully hard to find in 2022 though, so I'm using this, don't ask. My boots smell like the most interesting boots in the world.
Here's where I make a mistake; I made these boots for my dad, so I had everything planned for them to be on a Christy wedge sole. They were meant to be a lightweight walker boot. I was so committed to the idea that I found the first pair of Christy soles I had to be too small by about 1/4", so I ordered up another pair in the size up. One day I realised that the heel height on the Christy is too low for the heel height on my lasts, so I aborted that idea. I might try again later since I have a lot of Christy soles now, but I think I need new lasts with a lower heel height.
Here, fast forward, the shoes are lasted finally and I have a layer of cork in the forefoot. I use the Press n Seal wrap to make quick patterns for the filler needed.
Steel shank and filler added. Note the uppers are turned out around the forefoot for the stitchdown after pasting and lasting. This is my first go at a stitchdown construction boot. Learned everything for this here on imgur, as it were.
Midsole of 4mm 10oz veg tan leather is glued and clinch nailed on, and the turned out part gets holes awled for stitching. There'll be two row of sole stitches.
First layer of sole stitches done. Using waxed Serabond thread in tex 600 weight for this.
One row of stitches done, gluing on the outsole next. Notice all of the brass clinch nails in the heel and midfoot area holding everything together back there.
Using 12-14oz sole bend for the outsole, about 5mm thick and incredibly hard. Sheet metal snips are needed to cut it, and it's still a huge chore even then. In hindsight, the finished soles are a little too thick for my liking, but I had only these leathers on hand and they're kind of expensive to buy, so it is what it is.
Glued up, clipped up
Second row of stitches done.
Trimming the soles down and attaching the heels was very time consuming, as I used nothing but a knife and a rasp. Probably took 3 or 4 days of whittling away til I liked the shape. Once that was done, the Vibram half soles get glued and nailed on, and everything gets sanded smooth and flush, and then they're pretty much finished.
Close up on all of the hand sewing.
Got a small family of brown and beige thread growing here since the project started. I'll make some more boots soon.
item.dc:creator: bigcontribution
